evidence: tiled-decode-pool.ts line 523: bare `catch { break }` replaced with `catch (e) { if (DEV) console.warn(...); break }` — factory exception logged before break.
tiled-decode-pool.ts line 654: `catch (e) { if (DEV) console.warn(...); wiringOk = false; }` — original error logged before setting wiringOk=false.
tiled-decode-pool.ts line 791: `catch (e) { if (DEV) console.warn(...); }` — postMessage error logged; `set.add` correctly skipped on failure.
tiled-decode-pool.ts lines 403/410: `iv` declared outside Promise constructor; `if (iv) globalThis.clearInterval(iv)` added after Promise.race — interval cleared on grace-timeout path.

tiling.ts lines 95-102: `MAX_TILES = 1<<24` cap added; tilesX and tilesY each individually checked, then `numTiles > MAX_TILES` checked — throws PyramidError('JXTC_PARSE') before Uint32Array allocation.

decode-core.ts line 415-417: catch block now does `console.error('ensureIccProfile failed:', err); return null;` — ICC extraction failure is logged before returning null. Failure mode (silent null) addressed with diagnostic logging.
decode-level.ts line 391: `tileIdOf(t, source.tileSize, source.level ?? 0)` — hardcoded 0 replaced with `source.level ?? 0` in deadlineHit failedTileKeys loop.
tiled-decode-pool.ts lines 312-314 and 838-840: both prewarmAsync calls now have `.catch(e => { if (DEV) console.warn(...); })` — unhandled rejection eliminated.
manifest-validate.ts line 207: `if (height <= 0) fail(...)` added before aspect division — zero or negative height caught explicitly with clear error before division.
decode-level.ts line 497 (decodeTileBytesProgressive finally): `await drainOutcome.catch(() => {})` added before decoder.dispose — abandoned drainOutcome Promise awaited and error swallowed safely.

tiled-decode-pool.ts line 820: `void pool.destroy(0).catch(e => { if (DEV) console.warn(...); })` — destroy errors logged. Note: `pool = null` still set synchronously before destroy completes, which is the pre-existing design; the .catch() addresses the hiding of destroy errors.
decode-level.ts line 391: `tileIdOf(t, source.tileSize, source.level ?? 0)` — hardcoded level 0 replaced in deadlineHit loop. Also fixes contracts-014 which was a duplicate of this finding.

tiled-decode-pool.ts lines 1314-1325: cache-hit tiles now fire 'dc' stage (not 'final') before the miss decode pass runs. 'final' stage for hits is handled by the final decodeTilesParallel pass at line 1366 implicitly. Non-monotonic progress ordering from verified.json is resolved.
manifest-validate.ts line 163: `if (cbe >= bytes) fail(...)` — changed from `cbe > bytes` to `cbe >= bytes`; equal values now correctly rejected.

Failure mode confirmed dormant: `parallelEligible` guard in decode-level.ts excludes dc-only from pooled path, so the wrong cache key is never reached in practice. No change needed; dormant bug correctly left unfixed.

verified.json verdict was 'uncertain'; the dc-only pooled path is excluded by `parallelEligible` guard so the total=tiles.length*2 accounting is unreachable from dc-only callers. No fix applied; uncertain impact leaves this deferred.

tiled-decode-pool.ts lines 618-620 (commit a335474f): `for (let i = 0; i < excessEnd; i++)` with `excessEnd = idle.length - minIdle` — iterates coldest handles (index 0..excessEnd-1) for reaping instead of hottest. FIFO reap order restored.
manifest-validate.ts lines 117-121: `if (w <= 0) fail(...)`, `if (h <= 0) fail(...)`, `if (bytes <= 0) fail(...)` — positivity guards added after requireNumber calls.
test/tiled-decode-pool.state.test.ts lines 123-124: `tilesX: 2, tilesY: 1` added to makeTiledSource return object — TypeScript compile guard now enforced.
test/tiling.test.ts line 96: `expect(covered).toBe(region.w * region.h)` — full coverage assertion added to property test; gaps in tile union now caught.

manifest-validate.ts lines 23-26 + 64-76: `MAX_OPAQUE_KEYS=64`, `MAX_OPAQUE_DEPTH=4`, `MAX_OPAQUE_KEY_LENGTH=128` constants defined; `sanitizeOpaqueObject()` function implements depth/key-count/key-length caps. Used at line 244 for metadata field.
manifest-validate.ts line 103: `result.params = sanitizeOpaqueObject(requireObject(...))` — params field now sanitized with same depth/key caps as metadata (security-1 helper reused).
manifest-validate.ts lines 118-122: `if (w > MAX_DIMENSION) fail(...)`, `if (h > MAX_DIMENSION) fail(...)`, `if (bytes > MAX_BYTES) fail(...)` — upper-bound caps added for all level numeric fields.
manifest-validate.ts line 130: `if (!/^[a-fA-F0-9]+$/.test(contenthash)) fail(...)` — hexadecimal-only format constraint; colon injection into cache key strings now rejected.
manifest-validate.ts lines 84-86: `if (name.length === 0) fail(...)`, `if (name.length > 256) fail(...)`, `if (/[/\\:]/.test(name)) fail(...)` — length cap and path-separator rejection added.

decode-core.ts commit a335474f: `_iccCache = new WeakMap<object, Promise<...>>()` at module level; `_iccCache.get/set` replaces `(source as any)[key]` property stamp — enumerable property pollution eliminated.
tiled-decode-pool.ts line 871: `d.w > 0 && d.w <= 1000000 && d.h > 0 && d.h <= 1000000` — upper-bound checks added in parseWorkerReply; extreme w/h from compromised worker now rejected.
ADR draft written at undefined/sections/010/adr_draft/hand-rolled-validator-vs-schema-library.md — architectural decision on Zod/Valibot adoption documented; no implementation change.

tiled-decode-pool.ts lines 811-844: module-level `pool`/`poolFactory` still bare reads/writes with no creation-in-progress promise guard. Concurrent synchronous calls before any await can still both see `pool === null`. No mutex/serializing promise added.

tiled-decode-pool.ts line 352: `if (this.state === PoolState.Prewarming || this.state === PoolState.Active) return;` — early-return guard added; concurrent prewarmAsync calls that arrive after Prewarming/Active state is set are no-ops.
decode-level.ts lines 151-152: `buffersInFlight.add(outBuf)` immediately follows `if (buffersInFlight.has(outBuf)) throw` with no interleaving code — TOCTOU window eliminated.
Same as errors-5. `iv` captured outside Promise constructor; `clearInterval(iv)` added after Promise.race — interval leak on grace-timeout path fixed.

tiled-decode-pool.ts lines 986+993: abort check added both before `next++` (line 986) and after (line 993). One extra tile index is still incremented per coroutine on abort but immediately detected and skipped; progress accounting discrepancy is one tile, not N. Substantially addresses the failure mode.

tiled-decode-pool.ts: outer finally at line 1411-1413 covers `buffersInFlight.delete` for all return paths including liveHandles.length===0 early return. Releasing empty handles array is a no-op so p.release omission is harmless.
cache.ts lines 44-47: `if (typeof WorkerGlobalScope !== 'undefined') console.warn(...)` — runtime assertion/warning added; realm isolation contract documented and violations surfaced at load time.
tiled-decode-pool.ts line 582: `if (w.timer) globalThis.clearTimeout(w.timer)` — waiter timer cleared on early resolution in release(); ghost setTimeout after early resolve eliminated.
tiled-decode-pool.ts lines 1217-1227: tile geometry (col, row, gridTileX, gridTileY, gridTileW, gridTileH) computed once in cache-scan loop and stored in hits array; no duplication in stitch loop.
decode-level.ts line 211-226: single `plan.tiles.map()` call computes tx, ty, tileGeom, dist, id, dcKey, finalKey all in one pass; Phase 1 and Phase 2 both read from `item.tileGeom`.
decode-level.ts line 233: `const failedTileKeys = new Set<string>()` accumulated during phase loops; post-pass dedup allocation eliminated.
decode-level.ts: `tileBytesList` intermediate array eliminated; `extractTileBitstream` call folded into single `tilesWithBytes` map at line 211.
decode-level.ts line 229: `tilesWithBytes.slice().sort((a, b) => a.dist - b.dist)` with dist precomputed in the map pass — Schwartzian transform applied, O(n) distance computation before sort.
tiled-decode-pool.ts lines 1330-1333: `misses.map(tile => ({ tile, dist: ... })).sort((a,b) => a.dist - b.dist).map(item => item.tile)` — Schwartzian transform applied to pooled path miss sort.
tiled-decode-pool.ts line 240: `inIdle?: boolean` field added to WorkerHandle; line 720: `if (h.inIdle && ...)` replaces `this.idle.includes(h)` in _reapBound — O(1) idle membership check.

tiled-decode-pool.ts lines 447-450: `if (h.inIdle) { indexOf+splice }` — indexOf only called when inIdle=true (handle is actually in idle list). When inIdle=false the scan is skipped entirely, fixing the O(n^2) reapAllIdle case.
tiled-decode-pool.ts lines 55-61: `ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS` hoisted as module-level const Record — per-call object literal allocation eliminated.

tiled-decode-pool.ts line 391: destroy() path uses direct `h.pending.values()` iteration (fixed). Line 440: destroyHandle path still uses `Array.from(h.pending.values())`. Partial fix — the destroy() hot path is fixed but destroyHandle snapshot remains.
decode-core.ts line 485: `cache.set(key, pixels.byteLength === need ? pixels : pixels.slice(0, need))` — zero-copy when byteLength equals need; unnecessary copy eliminated.
decode-level.ts lines 223-224: `dcKey` and `finalKey` precomputed in single map pass at tilesWithBytes creation; N+1 cache.get() calls with per-call string construction eliminated.

cache.ts lines 38/57-66: `bytesIdCache = new WeakMap<Uint8Array, string>()` caches full composite string; `bytesId()` returns cached result on WeakMap hit — no template-literal allocation on repeat calls.
ADR draft at undefined/sections/010/adr_draft/perf-14-tile-decode-benchmark.md — benchmark approach documented; no implementation of benchmark CI gate.
verified.json marked 'confirmed' but fix_difficulty was 'complex'. No change found in current source — `handles.map(async (h) => { while (true) {...} })` pattern unchanged. Complex refactor deferred.
decode-core.ts line 11: `format: PixelFormat` (required, not optional) — optional marker removed; callers can now rely on format presence without type guard.

worker-protocol.ts: SAB load variant `{ v: 1; type: 'load'; bytesId: number; sab: SharedArrayBuffer; byteLength: number }` added to WorkerRequest union (commit a335474f). tiled-decode-pool.ts line 786: `as any` cast removed from SAB postMessage call.

manifest-validate.ts line 186: `parsePyramidManifest` return type is still `PyramidManifest` (includes schema: 1|2). Always normalizes to schema: 2 but return type not narrowed. `schema: 1` dead branch still type-valid for callers.

tiled-decode-pool.ts line 699-700: `const format: PixelFormat = job.bytesPerPixel === 8 ? 'rgba16' : 'rgba8'; job.resolve({ pixels, width: reply.w, height: reply.h, format })` — format field added to job.resolve.
tiling.ts lines 42-66: all `throw new Error(...)` replaced with `throw new PyramidError('JXTC_PARSE', ...)` throughout parseJxtcHeader, getOrParseJxtcTileIndex, and extractTileBitstream.

decode-level.ts line 627 in decodeWhole: `(res as any).iccProfile = icc` still present. Lines 419 and 453 use typed `result.iccProfile = icc` correctly. One remaining as-any cast in decodeWhole path not fixed.
tiled-decode-pool.ts line 1103: `pool?: PyramidWorkerPool` added to Uint8Array-first overload options — callers can now pass pool in a type-safe way.
No `validateWorkerRequest` dev-mode assertion found in current source. The contracts-002 fix (SAB typed union) reduces the surface, but no runtime validation of outbound WorkerRequest messages added.
ADR draft at undefined/sections/010/adr_draft/worker-protocol-version-negotiation.md — version negotiation approach documented; no implementation.
manifest-validate.ts line 152: `if (cols !== Math.ceil(w / tileSize)) fail(...)` and `if (rows !== Math.ceil(h / tileSize)) fail(...)` — cross-validation of cols/rows against w/h/tileSize added.
ADR draft at undefined/sections/010/adr_draft/errors-15-pyramid-error-code-open-union.md — UnknownPyramidErrorCode alias vs open union design documented; open union unchanged in source.

tiled-decode-pool.ts lines 546-548: `if (DEV && got.length < count) { console.warn('[pyramid] acquire timeout: requested N, got M, pool at max K') }` — diagnostic warning added when acquire timeout fires with partial handles.
Original plan status was deferred; no change. GalleryIndex.next format validation depends on consumer audit not yet completed.
